---
title: "Module Prototype: Decision Tree Analysis"
---

## Objective

Prototype a Shiny module that explains hotel occupancy classification with an interpretable decision tree and supporting diagnostics.

## Data Contract

Input dataset: `team/jin-qinhao/Take-Home-Exercise2/data/tourism_decision_tree_ready.csv`

Required fields:

1. `date`
2. `month`
3. `quarter`
4. `period`
5. `visitor_arrivals`
6. `visitor_arrivals_china`
7. `china_share`
8. `hotel_occ`
9. `avg_stay_monthly_capped`
10. `hotel_occ_level_tertile`
11. `dataset_split`

Derived fields used by the module:

1. `arrivals_million`
2. `china_share_pct`
3. `stay_days`

## Analytical Design

1. Load the prepared classification dataset.
2. Split observations into training and testing sets with `dataset_split`.
3. Derive presentation-friendly predictors for the tree view.
4. Train a classification tree to predict `hotel_occ_level_tertile`.
5. Tune and prune the tree using the minimum cross-validation error.
6. Generate an interpretable tree image and an interactive HTML tree.
7. Score the test set and compute confusion matrix, accuracy, kappa, and variable importance.
8. Return visuals and summary cards for the Shiny page.

## Parameter Exposure

| Parameter | UI Component | Default | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|
| `tree_view_mode` | `radioButtons` | static image | one of static / interactive |
| `prediction_rows` | `sliderInput` | 10 | min 5, max 20 |
| `prediction_filter` | `selectInput` | all rows | valid option only |
| `actual_class_filter` | `selectInput` | all classes | valid occupancy label |
| `top_variables` | `sliderInput` | 4 | min 1, max 6 |

## Output Exposure

| Output | Format | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Decision tree visual | image / HTML widget | Explain the split path and occupancy classes |
| Complexity parameter table | `DT::datatable` | Show pruning path and selected CP |
| Confusion matrix | `ggplot2` heatmap | Show where classes are mixed up |
| Prediction preview | `DT::datatable` | Inspect actual vs predicted records |
| KPI cards | styled value boxes | Highlight accuracy, kappa, chosen CP, leaf nodes |
| Key message card | text block | Summarize main split and hardest class |

## Implementation Files

1. `shiny/app.R`
2. `team/jin-qinhao/Take-Home-Exercise2/Take-Home-Exercise2.qmd`
3. `team/jin-qinhao/Take-Home-Exercise2/data/tourism_decision_tree_ready.csv`
4. `team/jin-qinhao/Take-Home-Exercise2/outputs/decision_tree_visual.html`
5. `team/jin-qinhao/Take-Home-Exercise2/outputs/decision_tree_static.png`

## Quality Gates

1. No missing target labels in the training or testing sets.
2. Tree rendering must work even when the source workbook changes column order.
3. Occupancy labels must be shown consistently as `Low Occupancy`, `Medium Occupancy`, and `High Occupancy` in the app.
4. The published Quarto page and the Shiny module must point to the same analysis outputs.
5. Rendered assets must be committed under `docs/prototype/` before review.
